About me
Data-driven from day one

Specialization

Aspiring Data Analyst with hands-on experience in Excel, Power Query, Power Pivot and dashboard development. Passionate about transforming raw data into actionable business insights through data cleaning, analysis and visualization.

What I focus on

I am currently focused on Data Analytics, especially data cleaning, data analysis, and dashboard creation. I have been working with Excel, Power Query, and Power Pivot to build analytical dashboards. Right now, I am expanding my skills in Power BI, SQL, and GenAI to become a well-rounded Data Analyst.

Projects
Featured work
Excel Power Query Power Pivot Dashboard
Superstore Sales Analysis Dashboard
End-to-end analysis of 4 years of retail data (2014–2017) covering ₹22.97L in sales, 5,009 orders, and 793 customers. Built a fully interactive dashboard with slicers for year and segment filtering, using Power Query for data cleaning, Power Pivot for the data model, and Pivot Tables for dynamic summaries.
Technology led all categories — ₹8.36L in sales and ₹1.45L in profit, the highest margin of any category
West region generated the highest sales (₹7.25L) and profit (₹1.08L) — nearly double the Central region
High & Medium discount orders generated losses of ₹76K and ₹58K — while no-discount orders drove ₹3.21L profit
Q4 was the strongest quarter every year, driven by November and December peak sales
View dashboard file
Power Query Power Pivot Pivot Tables Slicers Data Modelling Dashboard Design
Excel Power Query Power Pivot Dashboard
Employee Attrition Investigation Dashboard
Interactive HR Analytics dashboard built in Microsoft Excel to investigate employee attrition patterns and support data-driven retention decisions.
Domain Human Resources
Tool Microsoft Excel
Dataset IBM HR Analytics
Employees 1,470
Attrition Cases 237
Dashboard Type Investigation Dashboard
Business Problem
The HR department needed to understand why employees were leaving the organization. The objective was to identify high-risk employee groups and uncover patterns related to attrition so HR could make informed retention decisions.
Objective
Build an interactive HR Analytics dashboard to analyze employee attrition across departments, job roles, age groups, income bands, overtime, job satisfaction, and tenure.
Dashboard Features
KPI Cards Interactive Slicers Department Analysis Job Role Analysis Overtime Analysis Job Satisfaction Analysis Income Analysis Tenure Analysis Executive Summary
Research & Development accounted for the highest share of employee attrition (56.1%)
More than half (53.6%) of the employees who left the company had worked overtime
Nearly 47% of attrited employees reported being Dissatisfied or Very Dissatisfied with their job
Nearly half (47.7%) of the employees who left the company belonged to the Below 3K income band
Employees with 0–2 years of tenure accounted for the highest share of employee attrition (43%)

What I Learned
Converting raw HR data into meaningful business insights, designing dashboards around business questions, creating executive-friendly KPIs and visualizations, and communicating analytical findings through concise business insights.
